regions.name: Sensory Input
regions.shortDescription: The gateway for signals from eyes, ears, skin, and body.
regions.role: Collects raw signals and starts the flow of information toward routing and interpretation systems.
regions.exampleActivity: Light from a red apple becomes neural activity from the eyes.

regions.name: Visual Cortex
regions.shortDescription: Processes shapes, colors, edges, depth, and motion.
regions.role: Transforms visual signals into recognizable visual features before memory and decision systems add meaning.
regions.exampleActivity: Recognizes that the object is round, red, and apple-shaped.

regions.name: Auditory Cortex
regions.shortDescription: Interprets sound frequency, rhythm, direction, and speech cues.
regions.role: Turns sound waves into meaningful auditory patterns that can affect emotion, memory, and action.
regions.exampleActivity: Detects the urgency of a car horn while crossing a street.

regions.name: Thalamus
regions.shortDescription: A central relay station for incoming sensory information.
regions.role: Routes incoming signals toward specialized processing areas and helps regulate attention.
regions.exampleActivity: Directs visual traffic to the visual cortex and sound signals to the auditory cortex.

regions.name: Hippocampus
regions.role: Links new information with existing knowledge so experiences can be stored and later reconstructed.
regions.exampleActivity: Matches the red object with the stored concept of apple.

regions.name: Amygdala
regions.role: Adds urgency, familiarity, fear, reward, and emotional context to perception and decisions.
regions.exampleActivity: Marks a situation as safe, familiar, risky, or urgent.

regions.name: Prefrontal Cortex
regions.shortDescription: Plans, compares options, focuses attention, and makes decisions.
regions.role: Integrates sensory evidence, memory, emotion, and goals into controlled action choices.
regions.exampleActivity: Decides whether to eat the apple, ignore it, wait, or move.

regions.name: Motor Cortex
regions.shortDescription: Prepares and sends voluntary movement commands.
regions.role: Converts decisions into action plans for muscles and coordinated behavior.
regions.exampleActivity: Prepares reaching for the apple or stepping back from the road.

regions.name: Long-Term Memory Network
regions.shortDescription: Distributed storage of knowledge, associations, and experiences.
regions.role: Represents durable patterns across cortex that can be strengthened, weakened, and reactivated.
regions.exampleActivity: Stores that apples are edible, sweet, and familiar.
